steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: The maximum height is $31.36$ feet at $t=1.4$, so the vertex is $(1.4,31.36)$. A quadratic with this vertex can be written as $$f(t)=a(t-1.4)^2+31.36$$

steps.title: Use the launch height
steps.content: The ball is launched from ground level, so $f(0)=0$. Substitute $t=0$: $$0=a(0-1.4)^2+31.36$$ $$0=1.96a+31.36$$ $$a=-16$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The model is $$f(t)=-16(t-1.4)^2+31.36$$ which matches Choice A — $f(t) = -16(t - 1.4)^2 + 31.36$.
